CVE-2026-34197.body:
# CVE-2026-34197 — Apache ActiveMQ Classic RCE via Jolokia API

✅ **Verification:** Verified — 3 independent sources

**Date**: 2026-04-08
**Tags**: #cve #rce #apache #activemq #jolokia #jmx #critical #ai-discovered #horizon3
**Source**: X (@Horizon3Attack) — [https://x.com/Horizon3Attack/status/2041614428752286134](https://x.com/Horizon3Attack/status/2041614428752286134)
**CVE**: CVE-2026-34197
**Severity**: Critical
**Component**: Apache ActiveMQ Classic — web console (Jolokia API endpoint)
**Discovered by**: Horizon3 Attack Team using **Claude AI**

---

## Summary

A **remote code execution vulnerability** in the Apache ActiveMQ Classic web console, exploitable via the **Jolokia API**. The vulnerability is:
- Exploitable with **default credentials** on unmodified deployments
- Exploitable **completely unauthenticated** on certain configurations
- Discovered through AI-assisted vulnerability research using **Claude**

---

## Technical Details

### Attack Vector
The Jolokia API (`/api/jolokia`) provides a JMX-over-HTTP interface to the ActiveMQ broker. This endpoint, when accessible:
1. Allows invocation of arbitrary MBean operations via JSON HTTP requests
2. With default/weak credentials (admin:admin), attacker can invoke `exec` MBean methods
3. Certain non-default configurations expose Jolokia without authentication entirely

### Exploitation
- Target: ActiveMQ Classic web console, typically on port 8161
- Endpoint: `/api/jolokia` or `/jolokia`
- Method: POST request invoking ClassLoader or thread class loading MBean to load remote code
- Impact: Full RCE as the ActiveMQ service account

---

## Affected Configurations

- ActiveMQ Classic with Jolokia enabled (default in many versions)
- Default credentials not changed (admin:admin)
- OR Jolokia endpoint exposed without authentication

---

## Exploitation Indicators

- POST requests to `/api/jolokia` or `/jolokia` with `exec` or `read` operations
- Class loading from external URLs in ActiveMQ logs
- Unusual outbound connections from ActiveMQ service process
- ActiveMQ spawning child processes (shell, java -jar, curl, wget)

---

## Remediation

1. Apply Apache ActiveMQ patch for CVE-2026-34197 immediately
2. **Change default credentials** (admin:admin) if not already done
3. Restrict access to the web console and Jolokia endpoint (firewall, VPN)
4. If Jolokia is not needed, **disable it** in `activemq.xml`
5. Monitor ActiveMQ for unusual JMX/MBean invocations

---

## AI Discovery Note

> This CVE was discovered by Horizon3's Attack Team **using Claude AI** to analyze the ActiveMQ Classic codebase and API surface. This is another data point that AI-assisted vuln research is producing real, critical findings.

See also: [[ai-bugbounty-autonomous-hacking-guide]], [[ai-bugbounty-claude-zeroday-ghost-cms]]

---

## References

- Horizon3 write-up: https://horizon3.ai (CVE-2026-34197 ActiveMQ RCE via Jolokia API)
- X: https://x.com/Horizon3Attack/status/2041614428752286134
- NVD: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-34197 (when published)

## Summary
Oracle is warning about a critical PeopleSoft Suite zero-day, CVE-2026-35273, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. The flaw is being actively exploited by the ShinyHunters extortion group in data-theft attacks. Oracle has issued mitigations. ShinyHunters claims to have stolen data from over 100 organizations running PeopleSoft.

## Exploitation
- Unauthenticated RCE against internet-facing PeopleSoft servers.
- Tied to an ongoing ShinyHunters mass data-theft / extortion campaign.

## Remediation
- Apply Oracle's mitigations/patches for CVE-2026-35273 immediately.
- Treat internet-exposed PeopleSoft instances as potentially compromised; hunt for webshells, exfil, and anomalous DB access.
- Restrict external exposure; rotate credentials and secrets.

**Source:** [@IntCyberDigest](https://x.com/IntCyberDigest/status/2055041338152734854) — 2026-05-15 (99K views)

### Summary
@IntCyberDigest highlights a less-known edge case in Fortinet devices documented in advisory **FG-IR-26-128**. Rather than acting purely as a remote code execution platform, affected Fortinet devices can serve as firewalls in the exploitation chain — i.e. the attacker leverages the appliance's firewall plane (not just its management plane) as part of the attack surface.

### Reference

### Action Items
- [ ] Pull FG-IR-26-128 from the FortiGuard portal and map affected versions

- [ ] Re-test detection rules against this new exploitation pattern

# CVE-2026-40008 — Apache IoTDB Pipe Processor Unauthenticated Deserialization RCE

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** Critical — CVSS 9.8 (no authentication)
**Component:** Apache IoTDB — pipe processor (RPC interface)
**Impact:** Unauthenticated remote code execution via insecure Java object instantiation.

## Details
The Apache IoTDB pipe processor accepts a **fully-qualified Java class name over RPC and instantiates it** with no authentication and no allow-list. An attacker with network access to the RPC interface can trigger arbitrary class instantiation at the network level — a classic unsafe-deserialization / arbitrary-class-load primitive that leads to remote code execution.

## Action
- Patch to the fixed Apache IoTDB release once published; monitor the Apache advisory / NVD entry for exact fixed version.
- Restrict the IoTDB RPC interface to trusted networks; never expose it to the internet.
- Add network ACLs / firewalling in front of the RPC port as an interim control.

> Source: [X @SecAlertsCo](https://x.com/SecAlertsCo) — 2026-07-10 (secalerts.co/vulnerability/...)

CVE-2026-42530.body:
# CVE-2026-42530 & CVE-2026-42055 — Critical NGINX Open Source RCE (F5 OOB Patch)

✅ **Verification:** Verified — 7 independent sources

**Severity:** Critical — CVSSv4 **9.2** (both)
**Product:** NGINX Open Source / NGINX Plus
**Type:** Use-after-free (HTTP/3 QUIC) and heap-based buffer overflow (HTTP/2 proxy)
**Status:** Patched out-of-band by F5; unauthenticated, remote

## Summary
F5 issued **out-of-band patches** for two critical NGINX flaws, both remotely triggerable by an **unauthenticated** attacker and capable of leading to **remote code execution**.

### CVE-2026-42530 (CVSSv4 9.2) — HTTP/3 QUIC use-after-free
Use-after-free in **`ngx_http_v3_module`**, triggered when NGINX is configured with the **HTTP/3 QUIC module**. A specially crafted HTTP/3 session can **reopen a QPACK encoder stream**, causing a use-after-free in the NGINX worker process (worker restart/DoS). If **ASLR is disabled or bypassed**, the attacker can execute arbitrary code.
- Affects NGINX Open Source **1.31.0 / 1.31.1**; fixed in **1.31.2**.
- Mitigation: **disable HTTP/3**.

### CVE-2026-42055 (CVSSv4 9.2) — HTTP/2 proxy heap overflow
Heap-based buffer overflow in **`ngx_http_proxy_v2_module`** and **`ngx_http_grpc_module`**, triggered when `proxy_http_version` is set to **2** or `grpc_pass` is used to proxy **HTTP/2** traffic.
- Fixed in NGINX Open Source **1.30.3 / 1.31.2**, NGINX Plus **R37 P2.1 (37.0.2.1)** and **R36 P6**.
- Mitigation: remove `ignore_invalid_headers off`; reduce `large_client_header_buffers` size below 2 MB.

## Defender actions
- Patch to NGINX OSS 1.31.2 (or 1.30.3 for the HTTP/2 fix) / NGINX Plus R37 P2.1 / R36 P6 immediately.
- If patching is delayed: disable HTTP/3 (42530) and apply the HTTP/2 config mitigations (42055).
- Inventory edge/reverse-proxy NGINX instances using HTTP/3 or HTTP/2 upstream proxying — those are the exposed configs.

## Sources
> Source: [@TheHackersNews (X) — F5 Patches Two Critical NGINX Open Source Flaws](https://x.com/TheHackersNews/status/2067661885907030116) — 2026-06-18
- F5 advisory: https://my.f5.com/manage/s/article/K000161616
- The Hacker News: https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/f5-patches-two-critical-nginx-open.html
- SecurityWeek: https://www.securityweek.com/f5-patches-critical-high-severity-nginx-vulnerabilities/
- BleepingComputer: https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/f5-issues-out-of-band-patches-for-critical-nginx-vulnerabilities/

## Tags
#cve #2026 #critical #nginx #rce #http3 #http2 #verified

---
*Captured via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-06-19*

## Update — 2026-06-19 (additional X corroboration)
Further X amplification of the F5 out-of-band NGINX patch confirms the note above (no new technical detail):
- @akaclandestine posted a PoC reference: "CVE-2026-42530 NGINX RCE."
- @The_Cyber_News: "F5 Patches NGINX Vulnerability That Enables Code Execution and DoS Attacks" (cybersecuritynews.com).
- @UK_Daniel_Card (mRr3b00t) shared the THN write-up ("F5 patches two critical NGINX" flaws).

> Source: [@akaclandestine (X)](https://x.com/akaclandestine/status/2067661726321922548) — 2026-06-18
> Source: [@The_Cyber_News (X)](https://x.com/The_Cyber_News/status/2067584468274340137) — 2026-06-18
> Source: [@UK_Daniel_Card (X)](https://x.com/UK_Daniel_Card/status/2067714596589179248) — 2026-06-18

*Captured via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-06-19*

## Update — 2026-06-22 (clearer technical path)
The Hacker News published a follow-up clarifying the exploit mechanics of both flaws:
- **CVE-2026-42530** "comes down to an **HTTP/3 lifetime mismatch** that can leave a **freed stream pointer treated as valid**" — i.e., the use-after-free arises because a QUIC/HTTP/3 stream object is freed but a dangling reference continues to be used as if live.
- **CVE-2026-42055** "lets **oversized HPACK data write past its buffer**, causing [an] unauthenticated worker" overflow — confirming the HTTP/2 heap overflow is reachable pre-auth via crafted HPACK (header-compression) data exceeding buffer bounds.

No change to remediation: patch to NGINX OSS 1.31.2 (HTTP/3) / 1.30.3 (HTTP/2) or NGINX Plus R37 P2.1 / R36 P6; disable HTTP/3 and apply HTTP/2 config mitigations if patching is delayed.

> Source: [@TheHackersNews (X)](https://x.com/TheHackersNews/status/2068935559704129715) — 2026-06-22

*Updated via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-06-22*

CVE-2026-46316.body:
# CVE-2026-46316 — Linux Kernel KVM Guest-to-Host Escape (arm64)

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

## Summary
A **critical Linux kernel vulnerability** (**CVE-2026-46316**) enables a **guest-to-host escape** in **KVM environments on arm64 systems**. A proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit has been publicly released. Because the flaw lives in the in-kernel KVM implementation (not user-space components like QEMU), successful exploitation results in **direct compromise of the host kernel** rather than a confined user-space process — making it especially severe for multi-tenant / cloud arm64 hosts.

## Details
- **CVE:** CVE-2026-46316
- **Affected:** In-kernel KVM implementation on **arm64** (not QEMU/user-space)
- **Root cause:** Race condition in the **vGIC-ITS (Interrupt Translation Service) emulation** within KVM on arm64
- **Impact:** Guest VM → host kernel escape (full host compromise)
- **Discoverer:** Security researcher **Hyunwoo Kim (V4bel)**
- **PoC:** Publicly released (per source)

## Why It Matters
- Direct host-kernel compromise breaks the core VM isolation boundary
- arm64 server/cloud fleets (increasingly common) are exposed
- Public PoC lowers the bar for weaponization

## Recommended Actions
- Identify arm64 hosts running KVM with untrusted/multi-tenant guests
- Apply kernel updates addressing CVE-2026-46316 as soon as available from your distro
- Prioritize patching on shared/cloud arm64 virtualization hosts
- Where patching lags, restrict who can run untrusted guest workloads on arm64 KVM
- Monitor for the public PoC / exploit attempts against vGIC-ITS

## Sources
> Source: [Cyber Security News (@The_Cyber_News)](https://x.com/The_Cyber_News/status/2066350567556075820) — 2026-06-15
> Article: https://cybersecuritynews.com/poc-exploit-released-linux-kernel-vulnerability/

**Affected versions:** **12.2.1.4.0** and **14.1.2.0.0**
**Type:** Unauthenticated, network-exploitable remote compromise (no user interaction)
**Status:** Patched in **Oracle Critical Patch Update — June 2026**

## Summary
A maximum-severity flaw in Oracle WebCenter Sites lets a **remote, unauthenticated** attacker compromise the product over the network with **no user interaction**. Successful exploitation can lead to **full takeover** of WebCenter Sites and impacts confidentiality, integrity, and availability. Oracle addressed it in the June 2026 CPU; the WebCenter Sites component is widely deployed as an enterprise content/web experience platform, making internet-facing instances high priority.

## Defender actions
- Apply the **Oracle June 2026 Critical Patch Update** immediately to all WebCenter Sites 12.2.1.4.0 / 14.1.2.0.0 instances.
- Inventory internet-facing WebCenter Sites deployments; restrict admin and management interfaces behind VPN/allowlist until patched.
- Hunt for anomalous requests/web shells on WebCenter Sites hosts given the unauth, no-UI exploitation profile.

# CVE-2026-48062 — CodeIgniter4 Upload Validation Bypass → RCE

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** Critical — CVSS 9.8
**Component:** CodeIgniter4 (PHP framework) — `ext_in` file-upload validation
**Impact:** Under unsafe upload configurations, a malicious file can bypass extension checks, potentially leading to remote code execution.

## Details
CVE-2026-48062 is an upload validation bypass in CodeIgniter4's `ext_in` handling. When applications rely on unsafe upload configurations, an attacker can craft a file that slips past extension validation and, depending on how the file is stored/served, achieve remote code execution. FOFA-style reporting indicates exposed instances.

## Action
- Update CodeIgniter4 to the patched release.
- Do not rely on `ext_in` alone; validate MIME type, enforce an allow-list, store uploads outside webroot, and disable execution in upload dirs.

CVE-2026-48908.body:
# CVE-2026-48908 — SP Page Builder (Joomla) Unauthenticated RCE

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** Critical — unauthenticated remote code execution, reported **actively exploited**.
**Affected:** **SP Page Builder** (JoomShaper) extension for **Joomla**. Fixed in **6.6.2** (apply update).
**Type:** Unauthenticated arbitrary file upload → PHP webshell → RCE. No login required.

## Summary
An unauthenticated RCE was confirmed in the popular **SP Page Builder** page-builder extension for Joomla. The flaw lets any remote, unauthenticated actor upload arbitrary PHP files to the web root and execute code on the underlying server. SP Page Builder is one of the most widely deployed Joomla extensions, so exposure is broad across Joomla-hosted sites. Exploitation was reported in the wild.

## Why it matters
- Unauthenticated + arbitrary PHP upload = full site/server compromise with trivial effort.
- Mass-deployment extension → large opportunistic attack surface for web defacement, webshells, and pivoting.

## Defender actions
- Update SP Page Builder to **6.6.2** immediately.
- Hunt for newly created/modified `.php` files in upload and media directories; review web-server access logs for POST uploads to SP Page Builder endpoints.
- Deploy WAF rules to block arbitrary file upload to the affected component pending patch.
- If compromise suspected, assume webshell persistence — rotate credentials and review scheduled tasks/cron.

## Sources
> Source: [@SecAlertsCo (X) — CVE-2026-48908 unauth RCE in SP Page Builder for Joomla](https://x.com/SecAlertsCo/status/2069988146477957137) — 2026-06-25

CVE-2026-50292.body:
# CVE-2026-50292 — libinput CRLF Injection → Root RCE (CVSS 9.8)

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

## Summary
Critical **CRLF injection vulnerability** in **libinput** (Linux input handling library). Unescaped `phys` output injects arbitrary **udev properties**, enabling **arbitrary root code execution** without authentication.

## Details
- **CVE:** CVE-2026-50292
- **CVSS:** 9.8 (Critical)
- **Component:** libinput (Linux input device handling)
- **Affected versions:** libinput before 1.30.4 / 1.31.3
- **Root cause:** `phys` field not sanitised before being output into udev rules — injected newlines allow additional udev property/rule injection
- **Impact:** Arbitrary root code execution via crafted input device `phys` identifier

## Fix
Patch to **libinput ≥ 1.30.4** or **≥ 1.31.3** immediately. Check distro security advisories.

## Tags
`#linux` `#infosec` `#cve` `#rce` `#udev` `#libinput`

## Source
- `2063223889417711778` | @SecAlertsCo | 2026-06-06 | https://secalerts.co/vulnerability/CVE-2026-50292

CVE-2026-50548.body:
# CVE-2026-50548 / CVE-2026-50549 — Cursor IDE "DuneSlide" Zero-Click Prompt-Injection RCE

✅ **Verification:** Verified — 3 independent sources

**Severity:** Critical — CVSS **9.8** (both).
**Component:** Cursor AI code editor / IDE (all versions **before 3.0**).
**Type:** Zero-click prompt injection → sandbox escape → **OS-level remote code execution**.
**Research name:** "DuneSlide" (Cato AI Labs).

## Summary
Two critical RCE flaws in the Cursor AI IDE allow a **zero-click prompt-injection** attack to break out of Cursor's sandbox and run arbitrary code on the host OS. An attacker who can get injected instructions in front of the agent (e.g., poisoned repo content, MCP data, web content the agent reads) can overwrite critical system files such as the `cursorsandbox` binary, converting sandboxed commands into **unsandboxed RCE** and leading to full compromise of the host and connected SaaS workspaces. Cursor's maker states >50% of the Fortune 500 use the tool.

## Technical Detail
- **CVE-2026-50548:** The sandbox permits writes into a command's working folder. That folder is the optional `working_directory` parameter on Cursor's `run_terminal_cmd` tool; when the agent sets it to a non-default path, Cursor adds that path to the allowed-write list without validation.
- **CVE-2026-50549:** Before writing, Cursor resolves symlinks to confirm the real destination is inside the project. The bug is the fallback: when the check fails (target doesn't exist, or attacker removes read access from a path component), Cursor trusts the shortcut's in-project path instead — enabling writes outside the project.

## Recommended Actions
- Upgrade to **Cursor 3.0** (fix released 2026-04-02); every prior version is affected.
- Treat all agent-readable content (repos, MCP servers, fetched web pages) as untrusted prompt-injection surface.
- Constrain agent file-write scope and monitor for writes to sandbox/runtime binaries.

## Tags
#CVE #Cursor #AI #PromptInjection #SandboxEscape #RCE #DuneSlide #2026 #verified

> Source: [@SecAlertsCo (X)](https://x.com/SecAlertsCo) — 2026-07-05 (secalerts.co) · Enriched: [Cato Networks DuneSlide](https://www.catonetworks.com/blog/duneslide-two-critical-rce-vulnerabilities/), [The Hacker News](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/critical-cursor-flaws-could-let-prompt.html), [NVD CVE-2026-50548](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-50548)

# CVE-2026-53359 — "Januscape" Linux KVM Guest-to-Host Escape

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** Critical (VM guest-to-host escape, host kernel memory corruption)
**Component:** Linux KVM (Kernel-based Virtual Machine), x86 shadow MMU / nested virtualization
**Impact:** A malicious guest corrupts host kernel memory, breaking the fundamental isolation guarantee of virtualization.

## Details
A newly disclosed Linux KVM vulnerability dubbed **"Januscape"** (CVE-2026-53359) lets a malicious guest corrupt host kernel memory — a guest-to-host escape. The flaw sits in KVM's x86 shadow memory-management logic (shadow MMU handling of nested virtualization) and affects both Intel and AMD systems. It reportedly went unnoticed for nearly 16 years.

## Affected
- Linux hosts running KVM on x86 (Intel and AMD)
- Multi-tenant / cloud virtualization especially at risk

## Action
- Apply KVM/kernel patches once available for your distro.
- Prioritize hosts running untrusted guest workloads.
- Watch for the fixing commit in KVM x86 shadow-MMU code.

> Source: X @The_Cyber_News — 2026-07-07 (cybersecuritynews.com/16-year-old-l...)

## Summary
Improper input validation in the **Zoom Desktop Client, VDI Client, and Meeting SDK for Windows** allows an **unauthenticated** attacker to take over an account **via network access** — no credentials and no user interaction required. CVE-2026-5426.body:
# CVE-2026-5426 — KnowledgeDeliver LMS Unauthenticated RCE via Hard-Coded machineKey

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

## Summary
Attackers exploited CVE-2026-5426 in the **KnowledgeDeliver LMS** to achieve unauthenticated remote code execution by leveraging hard-coded ASP.NET `machineKey` values. The attack chain deploys the **Godzilla (BLUEBEAM) web shell** and delivers a **Cobalt Strike Beacon** on vulnerable internet-facing servers.

## Key Details
- **CVE:** CVE-2026-5426
- **Product:** KnowledgeDeliver LMS
- **Attack Vector:** Hard-coded ASP.NET `machineKey` → unauthenticated RCE
- **Post-Exploitation:** Godzilla / BLUEBEAM web shell + Cobalt Strike Beacon
- **Exposure:** Internet-facing deployments

## Attack Chain
1. Attacker obtains/guesses the known hard-coded `machineKey`
2. Crafts a malicious ViewState payload using the key (unauthenticated)
3. Achieves RCE via deserialization
4. Deploys Godzilla (BLUEBEAM) web shell for persistent access
5. Drops Cobalt Strike Beacon for C2

## Hunting / Detection
- Look for unusual ViewState-based POST requests on LMS endpoints
- Hunt for Godzilla/BLUEBEAM web shell IOCs in web directories
- Monitor for Cobalt Strike Beacon traffic patterns (default/custom malleable C2)
- Check ASP.NET app `web.config` for hard-coded `machineKey` values

CVE-2026-55200.body:
# CVE-2026-55200 — libssh2 Out-of-Bounds Heap Write (SSH Client RCE)

✅ **Verification:** Verified — 3 independent sources

**Severity:** Critical — CVSS **9.2** (per VulnCheck, as reported)
**Affected:** **libssh2** SSH client library — **every version up to and including 1.11.1**. libssh2 is embedded in a very large number of tools, language bindings, and applications that make outbound SSH connections.
**Type:** **Out-of-bounds heap write** in `ssh2_transport_read()` (transport-layer packet parsing) → memory corruption, potential remote code execution.
**Status:** Reported; verify availability of a fixed release (expected >1.11.1) and patch.

## Summary
A critical memory-corruption flaw was reported in **libssh2**, the widely embedded client-side SSH library. The bug is an **out-of-bounds heap write in `ssh2_transport_read()`**, the routine that parses incoming SSH transport packets. Because the defect is in client-side packet handling, a **malicious or compromised SSH server** (or an active man-in-the-middle) can send crafted transport data to a connecting client and corrupt heap memory — a primitive that can escalate to **remote code execution** in the process linking libssh2. The library's ubiquity (git tooling, language bindings, automation agents, network/management software) makes the blast radius broad.

## Why it matters
- Client-side trigger: any tool that connects out to an attacker-controlled or hijacked SSH endpoint is exposed.
- "Embedded in countless tools" — transitive dependency risk means many products ship libssh2 without it being obvious.

## Defender actions
- Inventory software and dependencies that bundle or link **libssh2**; identify versions **≤ 1.11.1**.
- Update libssh2 to the fixed release once published; rebuild/repackage downstream software that statically links it.
- Until patched, avoid connecting libssh2-based clients to untrusted SSH servers; pin known-good host keys to reduce MitM risk.
- Watch vendor advisories for products that embed libssh2 (git clients, automation/CI agents, network management tools).

## Sources
> Source: [@IntCyberDigest (X) — Critical RCE in libssh2 (CVE-2026-55200)](https://x.com/IntCyberDigest/status/2069320581221167597) — 2026-06-23

*Captured via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-06-23*

## Tags
#cve #rce #libssh2 #ssh #supplychain #2026 #verified

---

## Update — 2026-06-25: Public PoC code released
Multiple proof-of-concept exploits for **CVE-2026-55200** have now been published, moving this from "reported" to "weaponizable." Treat as **actively exploitable** and prioritize patching of any software linking libssh2 ≤ 1.11.1.

- PoC #1: `github.com/bikini/exploitarium/tree/main/libssh2-cve-2026-55200-poc`
- PoC #2: `github.com/0xBlackash/CVE-2026-55200`

With PoCs public, opportunistic exploitation against clients lured to malicious SSH endpoints becomes more likely. Re-prioritize dependency inventory and patching.

> Source: [@IntCyberDigest (X) — PoC released for libssh2 CVE-2026-55200](https://x.com/IntCyberDigest/status/2069410242392105046) — 2026-06-23
> Source: [@DarkWebInformer (X) — another PoC for CVE-2026-55200](https://x.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2069846786513023248) — 2026-06-24

*Appended via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-06-25*

## 2026-06-29 — Additional public PoC + cleanup emphasis

@TheHackersNews reports CVE-2026-55200 "now has public PoC code." Restated impact: a **malicious SSH server** can trigger client-side memory corruption with **no credentials and no user interaction**; affected **through libssh2 1.11.1**. Highlighted remediation challenge: the hard part of cleanup is **finding bundled and static copies** of libssh2 embedded inside other software, not just the system package.

> Source: X @TheHackersNews (tweet 2071493620872405067) — 2026-06-29

*Appended via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-06-29*

## 2026-07-01 — Supply-chain exposure elaboration
@TheHackersNews stresses the real exposed surface is **not just systems with a standalone libssh2 package**. libssh2 is a **client-side SSH library embedded in curl, Git, PHP, backup agents, firmware updaters, and appliances**. **Static/bundled copies can remain vulnerable even after a distro update**, so patching the OS package is insufficient — inventory embedded copies across tooling, firmware, and appliances.
> Source: [@TheHackersNews (X)](https://x.com/TheHackersNews) — 2026-06-30
*Appended via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-07-01*

CVE-2026-56141.body:
# CVE-2026-56141 — JetBrains Hub Account Takeover via Predictable Restore Codes

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**CVSS:** 9.8 (Critical) · **Status:** Patched · **Vector:** Account takeover (weak RNG)

## Summary
JetBrains Hub is vulnerable to account takeover through **predictable account-restore codes**. A weak random number generator makes the restore/recovery codes **guessable**, allowing an attacker to brute-force or predict a victim's code and seize the account without credentials. Tracked as **CVE-2026-56141** (CVSS 9.8).

## Affected / Fix
- Affected: JetBrains Hub (identity/SSO service fronting TeamCity, YouTrack, Space, etc.).
- Fix: upgrade to **2026.1.13757+** or the version-equivalent fixed build. Patch immediately given the 9.8 score and the central SSO role of Hub.

## Why it matters
- Hub is an identity provider for multiple JetBrains products — a single ATO can cascade into CI/CD (TeamCity), issue tracking, and source-adjacent systems.
- No valid credentials required; only a predictable code.

## Defender actions
- Patch Hub to the fixed build now; rotate restore/recovery codes and invalidate outstanding ones.
- Force re-auth / session invalidation; review admin and service accounts for unexpected logins.
- Enforce MFA on Hub-backed accounts.

## Sources
> Source: [@SecAlertsCo (X) — JetBrains Hub ATO via predictable restore codes (CVE-2026-56141, CVSS 9.8)](https://x.com/SecAlertsCo) — 2026-06-26

CVE-2026-57433.body:
# CVE-2026-57433 — Perl Storable Deserialization Integer Overflow (CVSS 9.8)

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** Critical — CVSS 9.8, no authentication required
**Affected:** Perl **Storable** module, versions **prior to 3.41**
**Type:** Signed integer overflow in **`SX_HOOK` deserialization** via `retrieve_hook_common`
**Fix:** Upgrade Storable to **3.41**

## Summary
A critical vulnerability, **CVE-2026-57433**, affects Perl's **Storable** serialization module in versions **before 3.41**. The flaw is a **signed integer overflow in `SX_HOOK` deserialization**, reachable through **`retrieve_hook_common`** when Storable deserializes untrusted (attacker-controlled) frozen data. Because Storable is widely embedded in Perl applications and caching/session layers that deserialize external input, an attacker who can supply crafted serialized data may corrupt memory — hence the CVSS 9.8 / no-auth rating.

## Why it matters
- Storable is a core Perl serialization primitive; many apps `thaw()`/`retrieve()` data that ultimately originates from untrusted sources (caches, queues, uploaded blobs, cross-service messages).
- Deserialization memory-corruption bugs frequently escalate from crash to code execution.

## Defender / researcher actions
- **Upgrade Storable to 3.41** across all Perl environments.
- Audit code paths that `thaw`/`retrieve` externally-influenced data; never deserialize untrusted Storable blobs.
- For bug bounty: hunt Perl services (legacy web apps, mail/AV stacks, monitoring agents) that accept serialized input.

## Sources
> Source: [@SecAlertsCo (X) — "Perl Storable (pre-3.41) critical CVE-2026-57433: signed int overflow in SX_HOOK deserialization via retrieve_hook_common, CVSS 9.8, no auth"](https://secalerts.co/vulnerability/CVE-2026-57433) — 2026-07-17

*Captured via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-07-17*

# CVE-2026-57572 — Crawl4AI Docker API Unauthenticated RCE

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** Critical — CVSS 10.0 (no authentication)
**Component:** Crawl4AI — Docker API (`browser_config.extra_args`)
**Impact:** Anyone with network access to the Crawl4AI Docker API can inject arbitrary Chromium launch arguments via `browser_config.extra_args`, leading to unauthenticated remote code execution.

## Details
The Crawl4AI Docker API accepts a `browser_config.extra_args` field that is passed to the Chromium launch command without sanitisation. An attacker can inject launch flags (e.g. ones that spawn processes or load attacker resources), achieving unauthenticated RCE on the host running the exposed API. CVSS 10.

## Action
- Upgrade Crawl4AI to **0.9.0** or later.
- Never expose the Crawl4AI Docker API to untrusted networks; put it behind authentication and network controls.

CVE-2026-63030.body:
# CVE-2026-63030 — WordPress Core "wp2shell" Pre-Authentication RCE

⚠️ **Verification:** Verified — multiple independent sources (Searchlight Cyber/Assetnote, The Hacker News, CybersecurityNews, SecurityWeek-class coverage) + X.

**Type:** cve | **Severity:** Critical (CVSS pending, pre-auth RCE) | **Date:** 2026-07-17

## Summary
A critical **pre-authentication remote code execution** vulnerability dubbed **wp2shell** was disclosed in **WordPress Core**. A single anonymous HTTP request can run code on an unpatched **WordPress 6.9–7.0.1** site — **even a default install with zero plugins**. The flaw chains a **REST API batch route confusion** (`/wp-json/batch/v1`) with a **SQL injection**, requiring no login, no special configuration, and no plugins.

## Key details
- **CVE:** CVE-2026-63030 (critical pre-auth RCE); companion **CVE-2026-60137** (high-severity) fixed in the same release.
- **Affected:** WordPress core **6.9.0–6.9.4** and **7.0.0–7.0.1**.
- **Fixed:** Emergency security releases **7.0.2** and **6.9.5**, published **2026-07-17**.
- **Root cause:** REST **batch API** route confusion → SQL injection → code execution. Reachable pre-auth on default installs.
- **Discovery:** Adam Kues, **Assetnote / Searchlight Cyber**.
- **Public test:** `https://wp2shell.com/` (checks if an instance is vulnerable).
- **Exploitation:** No in-the-wild exploitation observed at disclosure (per Searchlight Cyber), but mass scanning expected given default-install reachability and no CVE-signature yet for legacy scanners.

## Action / Mitigation
- **Update immediately** to WordPress **7.0.2** (or **6.9.5** on the 6.9 branch).
- If patching is delayed: block anonymous access to the batch API — block `/wp-json/batch/v1` and `?rest_route=/batch/v1` at the WAF, or install a plugin restricting anonymous REST access.
- Hunt web logs for POSTs to the batch route from unauthenticated sources; review for SQLi-style payloads.

## References
- https://slcyber.io/research-center/wp2shell-pre-authentication-rce-in-wordpress-core/
- https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/new-wp2shell-wordpress-core-flaw-lets.html
- https://cybersecuritynews.com/wp2shell-rce-vulnerability/
- https://wp2shell.com/

## Source

> Source: [X @IntCyberDigest](https://x.com/IntCyberDigest/status/2077145793333514593) — 2026-07-17

# SonicWall SMA1000 Zero-Days — CVE-2026-15409 & CVE-2026-15410 (Actively Exploited)

**Type:** cve | **Severity:** Critical (CVSS 10.0 + 7.2, chained) | **Date:** 2026-07-16

## Summary
SonicWall confirmed **two SMA1000 zero-days exploited in the wild since ~June 22, 2026**, discovered by the **Rapid7 MDR** team. Chained together they yield **unauthenticated remote code execution**.
- **CVE-2026-15409** — CVSS **10.0** SSRF in the SMA1000 Appliance **Work Place** interface; remote, unauthenticated; coerces the appliance into requests to unintended locations.
- **CVE-2026-15410** — CVSS **7.2** post-authentication **OS command injection** in the SMA1000 Appliance **Management Console** (authenticated admin → arbitrary OS commands).

## Affected / Fix
- Affected: SonicWall **SMA6210, SMA7210, SMA8200v** appliances.
- Fixed: hotfixes **v12.4.3-03453** and **12.5.0-02835** — patch immediately.
- **CISA BOD 26-04**: federal agencies must remediate by **2026-07-17** or discontinue use.

## Action
- Apply SonicWall hotfixes now; if unable, take SMA1000 Work Place offline.
- Hunt for SSRF probes and unexpected outbound requests from the appliance; review management-console command history / new admin activity.
- Rotate appliance admin credentials and session tokens post-patch.

CVE-2026-56196.body:
# Windows Admin Center RCE — CVE-2026-56196 & CVE-2026-58031

**Type:** cve | **Severity:** Critical (RCE) | **Date:** 2026-07-16

## Summary
Two **remote code execution** vulnerabilities in **Windows Admin Center (WAC)**, tracked as **CVE-2026-56196** and **CVE-2026-58031**, addressed in Microsoft's July 2026 update guide. WAC is a privileged web-based management console for Windows Server/cluster fleets, so RCE here can translate to broad fleet compromise.

## Affected / Fix
- Affected: Windows Admin Center (pre-fix builds).
- Fix: apply the July 2026 Microsoft security updates per MSRC update guide.

## Action
- Patch WAC gateways promptly; restrict WAC access to admin networks/jump hosts only.
- Ensure WAC gateways are not internet-exposed; enforce MFA on admin access.
- Review WAC audit logs for unexpected connections or command execution.

> Source: [X @hawktrace](https://x.com/hawktrace) — 2026-07-15
> Source: [MSRC update guide](https://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/) — 2026-07-14

CVE-2026-11405.body:
# CVE-2026-11405 — Tenda Firmware Undocumented Authentication Backdoor

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** High (hardcoded backdoor → unauthorized admin session)
**Component:** Tenda router firmware, `/bin/httpd`
**Status:** Unpatched at time of disclosure
**Impact:** Attacker who knows the backdoor secret gains an administrative session.

## Details
Several Tenda firmware builds embed an undocumented authentication backdoor in `/bin/httpd`. CVE-2026-11405 works as follows: after a normal login fails, the httpd binary additionally checks the supplied password against `sys.rzadmin.password`. If it matches, the daemon creates an admin session — bypassing normal authentication. Still unpatched.

## Action
- Restrict web-management interface exposure (LAN-only, no WAN admin).
- Watch for a Tenda firmware update removing the `sys.rzadmin.password` check.
- Consider replacing affected devices in high-security environments.

> Source: X @TheHackersNews — 2026-07-07 (thehackernews.com/2026/07/certcc...)

## Tags
#cve #tenda #iot #backdoor #router #unpatched #unverified

CVE-2026-11999.body:
# CVE-2026-11999 — wolfSSL OpenSSL-Compat X.509 Trust-Chain Bypass

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Component:** wolfSSL **OpenSSL compatibility** certificate verifier — `wolfSSL_X509_verify_cert()`.
**Flaw:** **X.509 trust-chain bypass via path-depth exhaustion.** An attacker can construct a chain that defeats verification, allowing an **untrusted certificate to validate** (TLS authentication bypass / MITM).
**Scope:** Affects only builds using the OpenSSL-compatibility verification path (`--enable-opensslextra`-style compat builds).

## Actions
- Audit products embedding wolfSSL with OpenSSL-compat enabled; update to a fixed wolfSSL release.
- Where feasible, prefer native wolfSSL verification APIs and enforce explicit chain-depth/pathlen limits.

> Source: X @CVEnew (tweet 2070208733297185197) — 2026-06-25.

## Tags
 #unverified

CVE-2026-13773.body:
# CVE-2026-13773 — IBM WebSphere eXtreme Scale SSRF

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** High (as reported)
**Component:** IBM WebSphere eXtreme Scale 8.6.1.x (affected 8.6.1.0–8.8.1.6).
**Type:** Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF).

## Summary
~50 CORBA stub classes in `ogclient.jar` call `ORB.string_to_object()` unsafely, enabling **server-side request forgery**. An attacker can coerce the WebSphere eXtreme Scale client into issuing attacker-controlled requests via the ORB transport.

## Recommended Actions
- Patch to a fixed version above 8.8.1.6 (or per IBM advisory) if running 8.6.1.0–8.8.1.6.
- Restrict outbound network egress from WebSphere hosts; monitor ORB traffic for anomalous destinations.

CVE-2026-20245.body:
# CVE-2026-20245 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Zero-Day (Admin → Root)

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 2 independent sources

**Severity:** High — exploited as a **zero-day** in the wild (per Mandiant).
**Affected:** **Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN** (confirm component/version matrix from Cisco advisory). Sibling to the previously-tracked CVE-2026-20262 SD-WAN Manager file-write flaw.
**Type:** Privilege escalation — turns **admin access into root** on SD-WAN infrastructure.

## Summary
Mandiant reports that **CVE-2026-20245** was exploited as a **zero-day** against Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN, allowing attackers to escalate from admin to **root**. Post-exploitation, the actors **restored configs, deleted traces, and hid a rogue "troot" account** — an anti-forensics + persistence playbook indicating a capable operator. SD-WAN controllers/edges sit at network chokepoints, so root on these devices yields broad traffic visibility and lateral movement.

## Why it matters
- Active zero-day exploitation + deliberate log tampering and stealth account creation = targeted, persistent intrusion.
- SD-WAN root compromise threatens the entire managed WAN fabric.

## Defender actions
- Apply Cisco's fixed release for CVE-2026-20245 as soon as available; check CISA KEV for due dates.
- Hunt for rogue/unexpected accounts (e.g., "troot"), unexplained config restores, and gaps in device logs.
- Restrict and monitor SD-WAN management plane access; enable centralized, tamper-evident logging off-box.
- Review related Cisco SD-WAN exposure (see CVE-2026-20262 note) for a combined patch sweep.

## Sources
> Source: [@TheHackersNews (X) — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN zero-day CVE-2026-20245 exploited to gain root](https://x.com/TheHackersNews/status/2070021613043757441) — 2026-06-25

*Captured via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-06-25*

# CVE-2026-20251 — Splunk Secure Gateway jsonpickle Deserialization RCE

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** High — CVSS **8.8** (as reported)
**Published:** 2026-06-26
**Component:** Splunk **Secure Gateway** (app powering Splunk mobile / Connected Experiences).
**Type:** Insecure **`jsonpickle` deserialization** → remote code execution.

## Summary
A deserialization flaw in Splunk Secure Gateway's use of **`jsonpickle`** allows crafted serialized objects to be decoded into arbitrary Python objects, leading to **remote code execution** on the Splunk instance. `jsonpickle` reconstructs arbitrary Python types from JSON, so untrusted input reaching its decode path is a classic RCE primitive. A public PoC was referenced (`github.com/reactivezero/C...`).

## Recommended Actions
- Inventory Splunk deployments with **Secure Gateway** enabled; apply Splunk's fixed version once the advisory is published.
- Restrict/inspect access to Secure Gateway endpoints; monitor for anomalous child processes spawned by the Splunk process.
- Review the referenced PoC to build detection once verified.

# CVE-2026-20896 — Gitea Docker X-WEBAUTH-USER Header Spoofing (Exploited ITW)

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 2 independent sources

**Severity:** High (authentication bypass) — **exploited in the wild**
**Component:** Gitea (Docker deployments behind a reverse proxy)
**Impact:** Reachable containers trust spoofed `X-WEBAUTH-USER` headers when reverse-proxy auth is enabled, allowing authentication as an arbitrary user.

## Details
CVE-2026-20896 affects Gitea Docker deployments: when reverse-proxy authentication is enabled, reachable containers trust a spoofed `X-WEBAUTH-USER` header, letting an attacker impersonate any user. First in-the-wild exploitation attempt was observed **13 days after disclosure**.

## Action
- Ensure the reverse proxy strips/sets `X-WEBAUTH-USER` and is the only path to the container.
- Do not expose the Gitea container directly; bind to the proxy network only.
- Patch Gitea to the fixed version.

> Source: X @TheHackersNews — 2026-07-07

# CVE-2026-24294 — NTLM Reflection Bypass → SYSTEM (Windows Server 2025)

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** High (privilege escalation to SYSTEM)
**Product:** Microsoft Windows Server 2025 (NTLM)
**Type:** NTLM reflection mitigation bypass → local/relayed privilege escalation
**Status:** Publicly disclosed with PoC exploit code

## Summary
A researcher publicly disclosed an NTLM reflection bypass (CVE-2026-24294) with working PoC exploit code that yields SYSTEM on Windows Server 2025. Reflection bypasses defeat Microsoft's NTLM reflection mitigations, enabling an attacker to coerce/relay authentication back to the same host for SYSTEM-level code execution.

## Exploitation
- PoC publicly available; bypasses existing NTLM reflection protections.
- Outcome: SYSTEM on Windows Server 2025.

## Remediation
- Apply the relevant Microsoft patch for CVE-2026-24294.
- Enforce SMB/LDAP signing and EPA (Extended Protection for Authentication); disable NTLM where feasible.
- Restrict coercion vectors (PetitPotam-style) and monitor for reflection/relay patterns.

# CVE-2026-28564 — Apache IoTDB REST Basic Auth Stale-Credential Replay Bypass

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Component:** Apache IoTDB — REST API (HTTP Basic Auth)
**Impact:** Authentication bypass via capture-replay of previously valid credentials.

## Details
The Apache IoTDB **REST API accepts stale cached credentials**, allowing an authentication bypass through **capture-replay**: an attacker who observes or replays a previously valid Basic Auth exchange can authenticate without holding current credentials. No privileges are required and the attack is fully remote.

## Action
- Patch to the fixed Apache IoTDB release; monitor the Apache advisory / NVD entry.
- Terminate the REST API behind TLS and an authenticating reverse proxy; rotate credentials.
- Restrict REST endpoint exposure to trusted networks.

CVE-2026-29509.body:
# CVE-2026-29509 — Das U-Boot NFS `nfs_readlink_reply()` Buffer Overflow

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** High (buffer overflow; requires malicious/compromised NFS server or MITM)
**Component:** Das U-Boot ≤ 2026.04-rc3 — `nfs_readlink_reply()` in `net/nfs-common.c`, when `CONFIG_CMD_NFS` is enabled
**Impact:** A malicious NFS server response can overflow a buffer during `readlink` handling, potentially corrupting memory / hijacking execution in the bootloader.

## Details
When `CONFIG_CMD_NFS` is enabled, U-Boot's `nfs_readlink_reply()` fails to bound the symlink data returned by the NFS server, allowing a buffer overflow. An attacker controlling the NFS server (or on-path) during a network boot can trigger the overflow. Affects boot-time trust, relevant to embedded/IoT devices that netboot over NFS.

## Action
- Update U-Boot past 2026.04-rc3 once patched; disable `CONFIG_CMD_NFS` if NFS boot is not required.
- Avoid NFS network boot over untrusted networks; pin/authenticate boot sources.

> Source: [X @CVEnew](https://x.com/CVEnew) — 2026-07-08 (cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2026-29509)

## Tags
#cve #u-boot #bootloader #nfs #buffer-overflow #embedded #iot #high #unverified

# CVE-2026-39352 — Frappe/ERPNext Unauthenticated Path Traversal (CVSS 8.7)

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

## Summary
Path traversal vulnerability allowing unauthenticated arbitrary file read on internet-facing Frappe-based ERP surfaces, including ERPNext.

## Metadata
- **CVE:** CVE-2026-39352
- **CVSS:** 8.7 (High)
- **Affected:** Frappe framework / ERPNext (internet-facing deployments)
- **Auth required:** No — unauthenticated
- **Impact:** Arbitrary file read

## Source
- **Tweet:** https://x.com/fofabot/status/2058751473165750711
- **Author:** @fofabot (FOFA)
- **Date:** 2026-05-25
- **FOFA Query:** `app="ERPNext"` — 115,700+ results found in past year

## Attack Surface
- Any internet-facing Frappe-based ERP (ERPNext most common)
- No authentication needed — one crafted path traversal request
- Risk: sensitive config files, credentials, internal data exfiltration

## Detection / Scanning
- FOFA: https://en.fofa.info/result?qbase64=YXBwPSJFUlBOZXh0Ig==
- Check for Nuclei templates targeting Frappe path traversal

## Mitigation
- Apply vendor patch immediately
- Restrict internet exposure of Frappe/ERPNext admin surfaces
- WAF rules for path traversal patterns (`../`, `%2e%2e`)

CVE-2026-41096.body:
# CVE-2026-41096 — RCE in Windows DNS Client

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

## Summary
Analysis of a **remote code execution vulnerability in the Windows DNS Client** (CVE-2026-41096 / PT-2026-40237). Client-side parsing of malicious DNS responses can lead to code execution.

## Technical Details
- Component: Windows DNS Client (resolver)
- Vuln ID: CVE-2026-41096 | Positive Technologies ID: PT-2026-40237
- Vector: malicious/crafted DNS responses processed by the client → memory corruption → RCE
- Detailed technical write-up published by @ptdbugs (PT)

## Impact
- Code execution on Windows endpoints that resolve attacker-influenced DNS
- Useful in MITM / rogue DNS / coffee-shop network scenarios

## Mitigations
- Apply Microsoft patch for CVE-2026-41096
- Use DNS over HTTPS/TLS to trusted resolvers; egress-filter DNS
- Monitor for unexpected svchost/dnscache behavior

## Sources
- @ptdbugs / Positive Technologies (2026-06-08) — tweet 2063954869845742028

CVE-2026-4342.body:
# CVE-2026-4342 — ingress-nginx Comment-Based Configuration Injection

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 2 independent sources

**Date**: 2026-04-08
**Tags**: #cve #kubernetes #ingress-nginx #rce #config-injection #high
**Source**: X (@kubernetesio) — [https://x.com/kubernetesio/status/2041698295647453575](https://x.com/kubernetesio/status/2041698295647453575)
**CVE**: CVE-2026-4342
**Severity**: High
**Component**: ingress-nginx (Kubernetes ingress controller)

---

## Summary

A **high-severity** vulnerability in ingress-nginx allows an attacker to inject arbitrary nginx configuration directives via **comment-based injection** in ingress annotations or controller inputs. This can lead to nginx configuration corruption, ACL bypass, or potentially remote code execution depending on the injected directives.

---

## Affected Component

- **Project**: kubernetes/ingress-nginx
- **Version(s)**: See [GitHub issue #137893](https://github.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/issues/137893) for affected range
- **Attack Surface**: Kubernetes cluster with ingress-nginx installed; attacker needs ability to create/modify Ingress resources

---

## Vulnerability Details

The flaw is a **comment-based nginx configuration injection** — specially crafted input containing nginx comment syntax can break out of the expected configuration context and inject malicious directives into the final nginx.conf file generated by the ingress controller.

**Attack Scenario:**
1. Attacker has namespace-level permissions to create Ingress objects (common in multi-tenant clusters)
2. Crafts an annotation or field value containing nginx comment escape
3. Injected nginx directive lands in the generated config
4. nginx reloads, activating the injected configuration
5. Impact: bypass upstreams, redirect traffic, expose internal services, or RCE

---

## Remediation

- Apply the patch referenced in [GitHub issue #137893](https://github.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/issues/137893)
- Review RBAC — restrict who can create/modify Ingress resources
- Monitor ingress-nginx admission webhook logs for suspicious annotation values
- Use OPA/Kyverno policies to block unusual annotation characters

---

## References

- GitHub issue: https://github.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/issues/137893
- X thread: https://x.com/kubernetesio/status/2041698295647453575
- 840 likes / 166 reposts — widely flagged by the Kubernetes security community

CVE-2026-43503.body:
# CVE-2026-43503 — "DirtyClone" Linux Kernel Local Privilege Escalation

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 2 independent sources

**Class:** Local privilege escalation to **root** via in-memory rewrite of `/usr/bin/su`.
**Technique:** A local user rewrites the **cached (in-memory) copy** of `/usr/bin/su`; the on-disk binary is **never modified**, so file-integrity / audit checks (AIDE, Tripwire, IMA) come back **clean** while a root shell is granted.
**Context:** Reported as the **4th bug with this same failure mode in two months** — an emerging Linux kernel page-cache / mapping integrity bug class. Closely related to [[CVE-2026-46331-Linux-Cached-su-Poisoning-Root]].

## Detection / Mitigation
- File-integrity monitoring is **insufficient** (disk binary unchanged). Hunt for unexpected `su`/setuid execution, anomalous memory mappings of setuid binaries, and child processes of non-privileged shells spawning root.
- Apply kernel updates as they land; consider `kernel.yama`, reduced setuid surface, and exec/mmap telemetry (eBPF/Falco).

> Source: X @TheHackersNews (tweet 2070503122691367089) — 2026-06-26; 2nd source X @The_Cyber_News (tweet 2070671911319925166) — 2026-06-27.

## Tags
 #unverified

CVE-2026-45504.body:
# CVE-2026-45504 — Microsoft Exchange Server File Read (SSRF)

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 2 independent sources

**Severity:** High — CVSS **8.8** (as reported).
**Affected:** **Microsoft Exchange Server** (e.g., Exchange Server 2016 CU23 referenced). Confirm full version matrix from MSRC.
**Type:** **Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) → arbitrary file read.** Requires an authenticated/authorized attacker per the PoC description.
**PoC:** Public — `github.com/hawktrace/CVE-2026-45504` + writeup at `hawktrace.com`.

## Summary
An SSRF in Microsoft Exchange Server allows an authorized attacker to coerce the server into making requests that yield **arbitrary file read** from the Exchange host. Public PoC code and a technical writeup are available, raising exploitation likelihood. On-prem Exchange remains a high-value target (mail content, NTLM relay, AD pivot), so file-read primitives frequently chain into broader compromise.

## Why it matters
- Exchange is a perennial top target; SSRF→file-read can leak secrets/config and seed further attacks (e.g., relay, credential theft).
- Public PoC shortens the window between disclosure and mass scanning.

## Defender actions
- Apply the relevant Microsoft Exchange security update once mapped to your build (check MSRC for CVE-2026-45504).
- If unpatched, restrict access to Exchange admin/OWA endpoints, enforce strong auth + MFA, and monitor for anomalous internal requests originating from the Exchange server.
- Hunt for unexpected file-read patterns and outbound requests from Exchange; review for indicators tied to the public PoC.

## Sources
> Source: [@DarkWebInformer (X) — CVE-2026-45504 Microsoft Exchange File Read, PoC + writeup](https://x.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2069854919859765699) — 2026-06-25

CVE-2026-46242.body:
# CVE-2026-46242 — "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel eventpoll UAF → Local Root

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** High (local privilege escalation, disclosed as 0-day)
**Component:** Linux kernel `eventpoll` (epoll) subsystem
**Impact:** Unprivileged local user escalates to root on Linux servers, desktops, and Android devices.

## Details
A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw dubbed **"Bad Epoll"** (CVE-2026-46242) is a race condition + use-after-free in the epoll subsystem. The UAF is in `ep_remove()`: it clears `file->f_ep` under `file->f_lock` but continues using the freed file object inside the critical section (`ep_list_del_rcu()` and `spin_unlock()`). Exploitation yields root.

## Affected
- Linux servers and desktops
- Android devices (Linux kernel)

## Action
- Apply kernel patches once available for your distro / Android vendor.
- Watch distro security trackers for the fixing commit in `fs/eventpoll.c`.

> Source: X @The_Cyber_News — 2026-07-04 (article: cybersecuritynews.com/bad-epoll-0-day...)

## Tags
#cve #linux-kernel #lpe #use-after-free #0day #unverified

CVE-2026-46331.body:
# CVE-2026-46331 — Linux Kernel Cached `/bin/su` Poisoning → Root

✅ **Verification:** Verified — 3 independent sources

**Class:** Local privilege escalation to **root without modifying any file on disk**.
**Technique:** Poisons the **cached copy of `/bin/su` in memory**; the binary on disk stays untouched, so **file-integrity checks come back clean** while the attacker already holds a root shell.
**Relationship:** Same "in-memory setuid poisoning" failure mode as [[CVE-2026-43503-DirtyClone-Linux-su-Memory-Rewrite]] (DirtyClone) — part of the recent cluster of Linux page-cache/setuid integrity bugs.

## Detection / Mitigation
- Do not rely on disk-based FIM. Use runtime/memory telemetry (eBPF, kernel auditing of setuid exec, anomalous mmap of `/bin/su`).
- Patch kernel promptly; minimize local untrusted code execution on sensitive hosts.

> Source: X @TheHackersNews (tweet 2070528444568645906) — 2026-06-26.

## 2026-06-29 — Public PoC released ("pedit COW" / act_pedit framing)

A public PoC named **`packet_edit_meme`** (author **Massimiliano Oldani**) was released for **CVE-2026-46331**. In these posts the bug is described as residing in the **`act_pedit` packet-editing action of the Linux kernel `net/sched` subsystem**, nicknamed **"pedit COW"** (CVSS 7.8). PoC repo referenced: `github.com/0xBlackash/CVE...`.

> **To verify:** This `act_pedit` (net/sched) framing diverges from the earlier "cached `/bin/su` in-memory poisoning" description captured for this same CVE id on 2026-06-26. Confirm against NVD / kernel advisory whether this is description drift on one bug or a CVE-id collision before acting.

> Source: X @DailyDarkWeb (tweet 2071240932213522803) — 2026-06-28
> Source: X @DarkWebInformer (PoC: github.com/0xBlackash) — 2026-06-28

*Appended via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-06-29*

CVE-2026-50691.body:
# CVE-2026-50691 — Windows BitLocker Encryption Bypass (0-Day)

⚠️ **Verification:** Reported — 1 source (Cyber Security News)

**Severity:** High (physical/hands-on access required)
**Product:** Microsoft Windows — BitLocker device encryption
**Type:** Protection-mechanism failure → full-disk encryption bypass
**Auth required:** Physical / hands-on access to the device
**Status:** Newly disclosed 0-day

## Summary
A newly disclosed Windows **BitLocker** zero-day, tracked as **CVE-2026-50691**, exposes encrypted devices to physical attacks that can completely undermine Microsoft's disk-encryption guarantees. The flaw stems from a **protection-mechanism failure in BitLocker's device-encryption workflow**, allowing an unauthorized attacker with **hands-on access** to a system to circumvent BitLocker and access data stored on the system drive.

## Context / Related
- Follows a June 2026 BitLocker bypass class from the NightmareEclipse cluster (WinRE + Defender Offline abuse) — see `GreatXML-BitLocker-Bypass-0Day-2026.md` and Guides/`bitskrieg-bitlocker-bypass-technique-guide.md`. Whether CVE-2026-50691 is the CVE assignment for that chain or a distinct issue is not yet confirmed.

## Mitigation
- Enforce **pre-boot authentication** (BitLocker + TPM **+ PIN**) to reduce offline/hands-on exposure.
- Physical-access controls for laptops/workstations; disable unnecessary boot/recovery paths.
- Apply Microsoft's fix once mapped to the July 2026 update cycle; monitor for unexpected recovery-environment boots.

## Sources
- [@The_Cyber_News (X)](https://x.com/The_Cyber_News) — 2026-07-15 — cybersecuritynews.com (CVE-2026-50691 BitLocker 0-day, physical access, encryption bypass)

CVE-2026-53565.body:
# CVE-2026-53565 / CVE-2026-53566 — Citrix Secure Access & Endpoint Analysis Client (Windows) Privilege Escalation

⚠️ **Verification:** Reported — single-source (CybersecurityNews via X @The_Cyber_News) summarising a Cloud Software Group advisory. Vendor advisory text not independently retrieved this sweep (RSS/vendor feeds unreachable from sandbox).

**Type:** cve | **Severity:** High (local privilege escalation on endpoint) | **Date:** 2026-07-18 (report)

## Summary
Cloud Software Group disclosed **two vulnerabilities** affecting **Citrix Secure Access Client for Windows** and the **Citrix Endpoint Analysis (EPA) Client**, tracked as **CVE-2026-53565** and **CVE-2026-53566**. Both enable **local privilege escalation** on Windows endpoints running the affected client software.

## Key details
- **Affected products:** Citrix Secure Access Client for Windows; Citrix Endpoint Analysis Client (EPA).
- **Impact:** local privilege escalation — a low-privileged local user or already-landed attacker can elevate to higher privileges on the endpoint.
- **Vendor:** Cloud Software Group (Citrix).
- **Exploitation status:** no public reports of in-the-wild exploitation at time of capture.

## Why it matters
Both clients are **widely deployed on managed corporate endpoints** as part of remote-access and posture-check enforcement. Client-side privesc on VPN/ZTNA agents is a favoured post-initial-access step: the agent runs with elevated service components, so a working exploit converts a phishing/infostealer foothold into SYSTEM without touching the network edge.

## Action / Notes
- Inventory endpoints running Citrix Secure Access Client and the EPA client; apply Cloud Software Group's fixed versions.
- Hunt for suspicious child processes or DLL loads from the Citrix client service paths.
- Confirm exact fixed build numbers and CVSS from the official Citrix/Cloud Software Group security bulletin before rolling out — this note is based on a secondary report.

## References
- https://cybersecuritynews.com/citrix-secure-access-vulnerability/

## Source
> Source: [X @The_Cyber_News](https://x.com/The_Cyber_News) — 2026-07-18

# CVE-2026-57992 — Microsoft Edge (Chromium) Use-After-Free RCE

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** High — CVSS 7.5
**Component:** Microsoft Edge Chromium engine
**Impact:** Remote attacker executes arbitrary code over a network by tricking a user into visiting a specially crafted webpage.
**Status at publication:** No available patch or public PoC exploit.

## Details
Microsoft disclosed a use-after-free memory-corruption flaw in Edge's Chromium engine, tracked as CVE-2026-57992 (CVSS 7.5). An unauthorized attacker can execute code over a network by luring a signed-in user to a malicious page. Distinct from CVE-2026-50521 (separate Edge UAF tracked 2026-07-02).

## Action
- Apply the Edge update as soon as Microsoft ships it.
- Advise users to avoid untrusted links until patched.

> Source: X @The_Cyber_News — 2026-07-07 (cybersecuritynews.com/microsoft-edge...)

## Tags
#cve #microsoft-edge #chromium #use-after-free #rce #unverified

CVE-2026-61857.body:
# CVE-2026-61857 — ImageMagick Heap Use-After-Free via XMP Profile Parsing

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** High (memory corruption via crafted image)
**Component:** ImageMagick < 7.1.2-26
**Impact:** A heap use-after-free caused by a missing null check when parsing XMP profiles. Attackers can craft a malicious image that, when processed, triggers the UAF — potentially leading to code execution or crash.

## Details
ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a heap use-after-free vulnerability caused by a missing null check when parsing XMP profiles embedded in images. Because ImageMagick is widely used server-side for automated image processing (uploads, thumbnails, conversions), a crafted image delivered through any such pipeline can trigger the condition without user interaction.

## Action
- Upgrade ImageMagick to **7.1.2-26** or later.
- In upload pipelines, validate/limit accepted formats and strip XMP metadata before processing where possible.
- Sandbox ImageMagick workers and apply a restrictive `policy.xml`.

> Source: [X @CVEnew](https://x.com/CVEnew) — 2026-07-12

## Tags
#cve #imagemagick #use-after-free #memory-corruption #image-parsing #xmp #2026 #unverified

*Captured via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-07-12*

CVE-2026-13221.body:
# CVE-2026-13221 — Perl Silently Incorrect Regex Matches (Trie Alternation)

**Type:** cve | **Severity:** Medium | **Date:** 2026-07-14

## Summary
**Perl versions through 5.43.9** produce **silently incorrect regular-expression matches** when an alternation of **more than 65535 fixed-string branches** is compiled into a trie. Because the mismatch is silent, regex-based input validation / filtering / security allowlists built on very large alternations could behave incorrectly (potential validation bypass or logic errors) without any error being raised.

## Action
- Upgrade Perl past 5.43.9 when a fixed release is available.
- Audit large generated regexes (>65k fixed alternatives) used for security decisions.

> Source: [X @CVEnew](https://x.com/CVEnew) — 2026-07-14 (cve.org CVERecord CVE-2026-13221)

## Tags

CVE-2026-20262.body:
# CVE-2026-20262 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Arbitrary File Write (Actively Exploited)

✅ **Verification:** Verified — 3 independent sources

**Severity:** Medium — CVSS 3.1 base **6.5** (vector AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N) — but **actively exploited** (CISA KEV, federal fix-by **2026-06-29**)
**Product:** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (formerly vManage)
**Type:** Directory/path traversal → **arbitrary file write** by an authenticated low-privileged remote user; can chain to **root**
**Status:** Patched — update to a fixed release

## Summary
A flaw in the web interface of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager lets an **authenticated** remote attacker (valid low-privileged account) create or overwrite files on the underlying OS via a crafted HTTP request, due to improper validation of user-supplied input during file uploads. A successful write can be leveraged for **privilege escalation to root**. Cisco reports it has been **exploited in limited, targeted attacks**, and CISA added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

## Exploitation indicators (from THN/Cisco)
Suspicious **WAR file uploads** to Catalyst SD-WAN Manager. Hunt in **`/var/log/nms/vmanage-server.log`** for path-traversal patterns such as:

```
../../../../var/lib/wildfly/standalone/deployments/suspicious.war
```

A `.war` deployed into the WildFly `deployments` directory is a classic web-shell / RCE staging primitive.

## Defender actions
- **Update to a fixed Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager release** immediately (KEV deadline 2026-06-29).
- Grep `vmanage-server.log` for `../` traversal sequences and unexpected `.war` deployments under `wildfly/standalone/deployments/`.
- Restrict and audit low-privileged web UI accounts; place the management interface behind VPN/allowlist.
- Review file integrity on the appliance and hunt for web shells / unexpected root processes.

## Sources
> Source: [@TheHackersNews (X) — CVE-2026-20262 exploitation indicators](https://x.com/TheHackersNews/status/2068602570449551427) — 2026-06-21
- The Hacker News: https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/cisco-releases-security-updates-for.html
- SecurityAffairs: https://securityaffairs.com/193693/security/cve-2026-20262-cisco-catalyst-sd-wan-flaw-under-active-targeted-exploitation.html
- cvefeed (KEV): https://cvefeed.io/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-20262

*Captured via x-bugbounty-intel-sweep — 2026-06-21*

# CVE-2026-42978 — Microsoft Use-After-Free Race Condition (Public PoC)

⚠️ **Verification:** Unverified — 1 independent source

**Severity:** Unrated at capture (confirm from MSRC/NVD).
**Vendor:** Microsoft (exact product to confirm).
**Type:** **Use-after-free race condition** with public PoC/research.
**PoC:** `github.com/grizzzer/CVE-2026-42978-PoC-Research`.

## Summary
Public PoC/research was released for **CVE-2026-42978**, a **use-after-free race condition** affecting a Microsoft product (PT ID: PT-2026-47904). UAF race conditions commonly enable local privilege escalation or RCE depending on the affected component. The precise product, attack vector, and severity should be confirmed against the Microsoft advisory once mapped.

## Why it matters
- Public PoC for a Microsoft UAF lowers the bar for exploitation (often LPE/RCE primitives).
- Race-condition bugs can be reliable with the right grooming, despite mitigations.

## Defender actions
- Map CVE-2026-42978 to the affected Microsoft product/build via MSRC; apply the relevant patch.
- Prioritize once impact is confirmed (LPE vs RCE); check CISA KEV for any exploitation signal.
- Review the public PoC repo for IOCs/behaviors to inform detection.

## Sources
